adequacy.rationale: Supported directly by Slide 2 LO and narration: "Recall and explain... 70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout." and "This 70% figure is the single headline statistic of the entire lesson."

source_refs.quoted_span: Recall and explain this lesson's single headline figure — that 70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout.

lo.statement: Recall and explain the lesson's headline figure: 70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout.

lo_delta.rationale_diff: Sharpened to an assessable action (state + explain) and anchored the exact figure to the source wording: Slide 2 LO line ("Recall and explain this lesson's single headline figure — that 70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout.") and Slide 2 narration ("70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout... This 70% figure is the single headline statistic of the entire lesson.").

missing: Concrete examples showing leadership decisions anchored to the single number
Stepwise description of how to anchor decisions to the number
Assessment prompts or criteria for a correct description
adequacy.rationale: Slide 1 asserts the anchoring principle ("we anchor everything to one decisive workforce number"), but does not explain how decisions are operationally anchored.

source_refs.quoted_span: we anchor everything to one decisive workforce number and what it means for clinical leadership.

lo.statement: Describe how leadership decisions in this lesson are anchored to one decisive workforce number.

lo_delta.rationale_diff: Narrowed "how" to a clear descriptive claim aligned with the source and made it assessable as a description, citing Slide 1 narration: "we anchor everything to one decisive workforce number and what it means for clinical leadership."

missing: Guidance connecting the 70% figure to staffing or workflow framing decisions
Examples or scenarios demonstrating application to staffing/workflow
Assessment rubric or model responses for applied decisions
adequacy.rationale: Slide 2 provides only the statistic ("70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout") and its prominence ("single headline statistic"), but offers no guidance or examples on staffing/workflow framing.

source_refs.quoted_span: every downstream decision about staffing and workflow is framed by that 70% finding.

lo.statement: Frame staffing and workflow decisions using the 70% burnout finding.

lo_delta.rationale_diff: Made the objective action-oriented (apply) while retaining the specific 70% figure from Slide 2 narration ("70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout.").

missing: Operational behaviors or prompts for maintaining focus on the statistic during decision-making
Criteria to assess sustained reference to the statistic
Examples contrasting loss of focus vs. proper anchoring
adequacy.rationale: Slide 1 states the focus principle ("we anchor everything to one decisive workforce number") but lacks process guidance for maintaining that focus across decisions.

source_refs.quoted_span: Keep your attention on that one figure; it carries the whole argument.

lo.statement: Maintain focus on the single statistic throughout the lesson to support decision-making.

lo_delta.rationale_diff: Converted a general focus directive into an observable behavior (consistently reference) aligned with Slide 1’s emphasis: "we anchor everything to one decisive workforce number."

adequacy.rationale: Slide 2 narration supports leading with the stat: "Say it plainly: 70%. This 70% figure is the single headline statistic of the entire lesson."

source_refs.quoted_span: When you explain this lesson to a colleague, lead with the 70%.

lo.statement: Lead with the 70% statistic when explaining the lesson to colleagues.

lo_delta.rationale_diff: Specified a concrete communication action (lead by stating) and used the exact headline figure from Slide 2 narration: "Say it plainly: 70%... This 70% figure is the single headline statistic of the entire lesson."
